problem_60.natural_language_proof:
Proof: let `φ : G ≃* G` be an automorphism. For any `x,y`, we have
`φ([x,y]) = [φ(x), φ(y)]`, so `φ` sends each commutator to a commutator. Hence it maps the subgroup
generated by commutators (the commutator subgroup) into itself. Applying the same argument to `φ⁻¹`
shows equality. Therefore the commutator subgroup is fixed by every automorphism, i.e. it is
characteristic.
